rationale: Y independently surfaces the version=1/2 integer-vs-string kernel loading defect, which closely tracks (and actually resolves) wtomin's exact ground-truth question that even the PR author had to clarify — a genuinely valuable, non-obvious catch neither GT reviewer nor X identified as a bug. Both candidates correctly find the flash_attn_varlen_func-None crash risk (matching RuixiangMa's GT comment) and flag weak test coverage of non-happy-path branches. However, X is more rigorous and precise: its line numbers were verified to match the actual diff exactly (e.g. line 108 RuntimeError check, lines 92-103 kernel cascade), it flags a real unguarded-third-get_kernel-call bug and an insufficiently-strict platform gate check, and it explicitly validates rather than assumes several claims. Y's citations for most findings (other than #1) come from a self-acknowledged diverged 'post-merge main' checkout, so its line numbers don't map cleanly onto the diff under review, denting actionability/precision despite the underlying claims being substantively sound.
